message: bd-gvuqn freeze RWF_HIPRI write hint parity

files.patch:
@@ -681,6 +681,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-gqvss","title":"add mounted-path btrfs symlink target PATH_MAX boundary and ENAMETOOLONG coverage","description":"Survey finding: btrfs mounted-path FUSE E2E covers symlink creation/follow, while ffs-core separately enforces the public PATH_MAX boundary for symlink targets. Scope: add mounted-path btrfs probes that accept a target exactly at PATH_MAX, reject an over-limit target with ENAMETOOLONG, and verify failed creation leaves no orphan entry. Acceptance: fuse_e2e freezes the mounted-path btrfs symlink target-length contract instead of relying only on core-unit coverage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:00:26.164538258Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:26:46.440231005Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T16:26:46.437876694Z","close_reason":"btrfs_fuse_symlink_target_path_max_boundary_and_enametoolong_contract at crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs:9074 (commit 92c30f3) freezes btrfs PATH_MAX symlink contract via shared helper assert_symlink_target_path_max_contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-gsk2c","title":"add ffs-core fuzz target for ext4 crash-state and journal-replay mount boundary","description":"Apply /testing-fuzzing to the ext4 crash-state and journal-replay mount boundary in `ffs-core`.

## Target surface
- `OpenFs::from_device(...)` / `open_with_options(...)`
- `detect_and_recover_crash()` at `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:2755`
- ext4 journal replay integration around mount open (`maybe_replay_ext4_journal`, `ext4_journal_replay`, `crash_recovery`)
- external-journal / replay-mode combinations that decide whether mount proceeds, degrades, or errors

## Why this matters
The repo already fuzzes lower-level JBD2 replay, but not the `ffs-core` integration boundary where superblock state bits, replay policy, journal outcome, and recovery-report exposure interact. This is exactly where mount-time recovery regressions hide.

## Scope
- Add a fuzz target that mutates minimally-valid ext4 images plus replay-mode / external-journal knobs and opens through `ffs-core`.
- Seed from current clean/dirty/error/orphan state fixtures and journal replay tests.
- Assert:
  - no panic / no hang
  - `crash_recovery()` and `ext4_journal_replay()` remain deterministic for the same input
  - recovery reports never claim replay counts larger than available journal metadata
  - skip-validation mode never triggers unintended mount-time recovery work
  - malformed external-journal combinations fail gracefully with stable errno/error class

## Acceptance
- New fuzz target registered in `fuzz/Cargo.toml`
- Seed corpus added from existing ext4 recovery fixtures
- Crashers minimized and promoted to regression coverage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:25:27.486078483Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:26:18.267452245Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T16:26:18.265193825Z","close_reason":"Covered by fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_openfs_journal_replay.rs (29KB umbrella harness): exercises OpenFs::open_with_options with Ext4JournalReplayMode, ExternalJournalInfo, Ext4FastCommitReplayEvidence, EXT4_ORPHAN_FS, EXT4_LAST_ORPHAN_OFFSET, JOURNAL_FAST_COMMIT_BLOCK, WalRecoveryReport — covers internal/external journal, fast-commit, orphan cleanup, and MVCC WAL replay through the real mount/open boundary as bd-p8c4q's acceptance requires.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","ffs-core","fuzz","journal","recovery","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-gto9j","title":"add mounted-path ext4 write-to-directory EISDIR coverage","description":"Survey finding: ext4 mounted-path FUSE E2E covers regular-file write and append behavior, but the operator-visible refusal for writing directly to a directory is only frozen in ffs-core unit tests. Scope: add an ext4 mounted-path probe that opens the root or a created directory for writing, asserts exact EISDIR, and verifies directory entries remain unchanged. Acceptance: fuse_e2e freezes the ext4 write-to-directory public contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:15:02.912544030Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T01:29:14.280124692Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T01:29:14.277587565Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-gvuqn","title":"CONFORMANCE: freeze RWF_HIPRI/FUSE_WRITE_CACHE write flag as non-mutating hint","description":"Queue-empty porting-to-rust fallback for FUSE WRITE flag parity. RWF_HIPRI shares bit 0 with FUSE_WRITE_CACHE in the exposed write_flags surface, and FrankenFS does not provide a separate high-priority polling path. Freeze the V1 contract that this bit remains a harmless write hint, not a sync request, NOWAIT path, or unsupported-intent rejection; add focused ffs-fuse coverage and update FEATURE_PARITY.md evidence.","notes":"Claimed by Codex for porting-to-rust queue-empty parity fallback. Agent Mail health is red due missing schema tables, so coordination is recorded in Beads only.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T23:20:28.459134606Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T23:32:10.979522564Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T23:32:10.977671589Z","close_reason":"Added focused ffs-fuse conformance coverage for the RWF_HIPRI/FUSE_WRITE_CACHE bit-0 collision as a harmless write hint, updated parity evidence, and validated with fmt, diff check, targeted tests, check, clippy, parity test, and UBS.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-gwfj","title":"Add btrfs chunk item adversarial parser coverage","description":"Fallback testing-fuzzing parity round: add deterministic adversarial regression coverage for ffs-btrfs chunk-tree item parsing. Cover valid multi-stripe payloads, fixed-header truncation, declared stripe-count payload truncation, zero stripes, zero chunk length, zero stripe length, and multiple RAID profile bit rejection; update conformance/COVERAGE.md.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T07:55:05.362372454Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T08:05:45.836955732Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T08:05:45.834545421Z","close_reason":"Completed btrfs chunk item adversarial parser coverage; focused parse_chunk_item tests, workspace check, and workspace clippy passed via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","fuzz","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-gznge","title":"fuzz: add fuzz_btrfs_xattr_items target","description":"ffs_btrfs::parse_xattr_items (lib.rs:294) - add to fuzz_btrfs_tree_items.rs","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:47:01.419335825Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:23:54.574Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T00:23:54.574Z","close_reason":"Already implemented in fuzz_btrfs_tree_items with parse_xattr_items determinism and xattr invariant coverage; verified with remote cargo check and clippy","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-h09f7","title":"add mounted-path btrfs rename missing-source ENOENT coverage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:54:04.897689735Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T03:36:24.701140887Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T03:36:24.701140887Z","close_reason":"Added mounted-path btrfs rename missing-source ENOENT coverage; verified with rch cargo test -p ffs-harness btrfs_fuse_rename_missing_source_reports_enoent -- --nocapture and rch cargo check --all-targets. Workspace clippy/fmt still stop on unrelated pre-existing ffs-tui lifetime and bench import-order drift.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -285,7 +285,7 @@ behavior of that operation, not whether the row is unimplemented.
 | FUSE readlink | FrankenFS spec §9 | ✅ | `FsOps::readlink` via `OpenFs` |
 | FUSE mount runtime | FrankenFS spec §9 | ✅ | Production runtime lifecycle, signal handling, dispatch coverage, and CI-safe skip behavior are implemented; OQ4 (`bd-h6nz.6.4`) is resolved for V1.x with explicit `flush` (non-durable) vs `fsync`/`fsyncdir` (durability boundary) contract and writeback-cache-disabled policy. `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now emits `SCENARIO_RESULT` coverage for ext4 (`ext4_rw_flush`, `ext4_rw_fdatasync`, `ext4_rw_fsync`, `ext4_rw_fsyncdir`, `ext4_rw_seek_data_hole`, `ext4_rw_seek_hole_fully_allocated`, `ext4_rw_seek_leading_hole`, `ext4_rw_seek_all_hole`) and btrfs (`btrfs_rw_flush`, `btrfs_rw_fdatasync`, `btrfs_rw_fsync`, `btrfs_rw_fsyncdir`, `btrfs_rw_seek_data_hole`, `btrfs_rw_seek_hole_fully_allocated`, `btrfs_rw_seek_leading_hole`, `btrfs_rw_seek_all_hole`) through real file flushes, file- and directory-FD sync calls, and mounted-path `SEEK_DATA`/`SEEK_HOLE` probes for punched-hole, fully allocated, leading-hole, and all-hole layouts with EOF `ENXIO` verification; btrfs read-only mounted-path `fsync`, `fdatasync`, and `fsyncdir` now also freeze exact `EROFS` with no data or dirent drift. |
 | FUSE ABI 7.40 protocol surface | FUSE kernel ABI 7.40 / libfuse 3.x | ✅ | Workspace builds the vendored `fuser` crate with `abi-7-40`, and `FrankenFuse::init` now negotiates `FUSE_SPLICE_READ`, `FUSE_SPLICE_WRITE`, `FUSE_SPLICE_MOVE`, and `FUSE_PASSTHROUGH` with stack depth 1 when the kernel advertises support. The vendored `fuser` protocol layer now exposes opcode 52 (`FUSE_STATX`), parses `fuse_statx_in`, replies with Linux statx-compatible `fuse_statx_out`, and preserves `FileAttr::crtime` as `STATX_BTIME`; `FrankenFuse::statx` routes that opcode through the existing `getattr` backend. The vendored ABI surface also exposes `FUSE_WRITE_KILL_SUIDGID`/legacy `FUSE_WRITE_KILL_PRIV` plus Linux `RWF_*` constants for write-intent propagation. Unit coverage freezes ABI constants, STATX request parsing, birth-time reply encoding, and mounted splice pipe-to-file transfer. |
-| FUSE pwritev2/io_uring RWF write intent propagation | `pwritev2(2)`, FUSE `WRITE` request flags | ✅ | `FrankenFuse::write` now decodes synchronous write intent from the FUSE `flags` field (`O_DSYNC`/`O_SYNC`) and routes it through `FsOps::fsync` after the normal MVCC commit, so `RWF_DSYNC` uses a data-sync boundary and `RWF_SYNC` uses a full sync boundary. The implementation deliberately does not treat `fuse_write_in.write_flags` bit 1 as `RWF_DSYNC`, because that bit is already `FUSE_WRITE_LOCKOWNER`; unit coverage freezes that collision and verifies no accidental fsync. `RWF_NOWAIT` uses the non-conflicting RWF bit to attempt inode mutation-lock acquisition without blocking and returns `EAGAIN` when the lock is already held. `RWF_APPEND` and `O_APPEND` resolve the write offset to current EOF under the inode mutation guard, while `RWF_NOAPPEND` preserves the caller offset on an append-open file. Unsupported non-conflicting write intents that require guarantees FrankenFS does not provide yet (`RWF_ATOMIC` torn-write protection and `RWF_DONTCACHE` cache-bypass/drop semantics) reject with `EOPNOTSUPP` before backend mutation. `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs` unit tests cover DSync, Sync, collision, NOWAIT fast-fail, append, NOAPPEND, and unsupported-intent rejection behavior, while `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` adds mounted-path `os.pwritev(...)` probes for both `RWF_DSYNC` and `RWF_SYNC`, proving requested bytes become immediately readable and survive remount when the host permits FUSE. |
+| FUSE pwritev2/io_uring RWF write intent propagation | `pwritev2(2)`, FUSE `WRITE` request flags | ✅ | `FrankenFuse::write` now decodes synchronous write intent from the FUSE `flags` field (`O_DSYNC`/`O_SYNC`) and routes it through `FsOps::fsync` after the normal MVCC commit, so `RWF_DSYNC` uses a data-sync boundary and `RWF_SYNC` uses a full sync boundary. The implementation deliberately does not treat `fuse_write_in.write_flags` bit 1 as `RWF_DSYNC`, because that bit is already `FUSE_WRITE_LOCKOWNER`; unit coverage freezes that collision and verifies no accidental fsync. `RWF_HIPRI` is treated as a harmless priority/cache hint because bit 0 is also `FUSE_WRITE_CACHE`; coverage freezes that it preserves the normal write contract without sync, NOWAIT, append, or unsupported-intent side effects. `RWF_NOWAIT` uses the non-conflicting RWF bit to attempt inode mutation-lock acquisition without blocking and returns `EAGAIN` when the lock is already held. `RWF_APPEND` and `O_APPEND` resolve the write offset to current EOF under the inode mutation guard, while `RWF_NOAPPEND` preserves the caller offset on an append-open file. Unsupported non-conflicting write intents that require guarantees FrankenFS does not provide yet (`RWF_ATOMIC` torn-write protection and `RWF_DONTCACHE` cache-bypass/drop semantics) reject with `EOPNOTSUPP` before backend mutation. `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs` unit tests cover DSync, Sync, flag-collision hints, NOWAIT fast-fail, append, NOAPPEND, and unsupported-intent rejection behavior, while `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` adds mounted-path `os.pwritev(...)` probes for both `RWF_DSYNC` and `RWF_SYNC`, proving requested bytes become immediately readable and survive remount when the host permits FUSE. |
 | FUSE inotify rename + dentry notification lifecycle | `fs/notify/fsnotify.c`, `fs/notify/inotify/inotify_user.c`, FUSE notify protocol | ✅ | `FrankenFuse` captures a `fuser::Notifier` during blocking, background, and managed mount setup, then issues `FUSE_NOTIFY_INVAL_ENTRY` for successfully committed `unlink`, `rmdir`, and both old/new directory entries after `rename`. Plain renames deliberately avoid `FUSE_NOTIFY_DELETE`, preserving the kernel/VFS `IN_MOVED_FROM` + `IN_MOVED_TO` cookie-pair contract instead of synthesizing delete/create observations. `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now carries mounted-path inotify probes for 100 same-parent renames and a cross-directory rename; both assert non-zero matching cookies and reject `IN_CREATE`, `IN_DELETE`, `IN_Q_OVERFLOW`, and `IN_IGNORED` during plain rename bursts, with the standard CI soft-skip when the current host denies FUSE mounting. |
 | FUSE ioctl FIEMAP | `include/linux/fiemap.h` | ✅ | `FsOps::fiemap` queries ext4 extent tree via `collect_extents_with_scope` and btrfs extent data items via `btrfs_fiemap_extent_items`. FUSE `ioctl` handler parses `FS_IOC_FIEMAP` (0xC020660B), rejects unsupported request flags with `EBADR`, honors `FIEMAP_FLAG_SYNC` by fsyncing writable mounts before extent lookup, and marshals the fiemap header plus extent array. `crates/ffs-core` now has deterministic btrfs coverage for inline extents (`physical=0`, `FIEMAP_EXTENT_LAST`) and keep-size prealloc extents (`FIEMAP_EXTENT_UNWRITTEN`), while `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` verifies mounted-path FIEMAP reporting for ext4 regular files, ext4 `SYNC` and invalid-request-flag contracts, plus btrfs regular, inline, and keep-size prealloc files. Note: FUSE transport may not deliver this ioctl to userspace on all kernel versions. |
 | FUSE ioctl EXT4_IOC_GETFLAGS | `fs/ext4/ioctl.c` | ✅ | `FsOps::get_inode_flags` returns raw ext4 `i_flags` field. FUSE `ioctl` handler dispatches `EXT4_IOC_GETFLAGS` (0x80086601). Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now verifies mounted-path `GETFLAGS` on a writable ext4 file. |
